description: Add missing unit tests for plugin SDK fetch utilities (postForm, postFormData, deleteJSON, parseRetryAfterMs) and storage (setSessionStorage) — functions that exist in tested files but lack individual test coverage.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for untested fetch.ts HTTP verb functions and parseRetryAfterMs
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the untested HTTP verb functions and retry-after parsing in fetch.ts so that all exported fetch utilities are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover postForm: sends URL-encoded body with correct Content-Type header, merges custom headers, returns parsed JSON response
Tests cover postFormData: sends FormData body WITHOUT setting Content-Type (browser sets boundary), returns parsed JSON
Tests cover putJSON: sends PUT request with JSON body and correct Content-Type
Tests cover patchJSON: sends PATCH request with JSON body and correct Content-Type
Tests cover deleteJSON: sends DELETE request, returns undefined for 204 response, returns parsed JSON for other statuses
Tests cover parseRetryAfterMs: parses seconds (e.g. '60' → 60000), parses HTTP-date format, returns undefined for invalid strings, returns undefined for negative values
Tests use the same real HTTP test server pattern as existing fetch tests (not mocks)
Test file is platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.test.ts (extend existing file)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts. The existing test file platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.test.ts already uses a real HTTP server (node:http createServer) for testing fetch functions — follow the exact same pattern. postForm (line ~200) sets Content-Type to application/x-www-form-urlencoded and uses URLSearchParams for the body. postFormData (line ~241) intentionally does NOT set Content-Type. putJSON (line ~281), patchJSON (line ~321), deleteJSON (line ~356) are structural variants of postJSON. parseRetryAfterMs (line ~37) parses both seconds and HTTP-date formats. Add new describe blocks to the existing test file. The HTTP-date branch uses `new Date(value).getTime()` — test with a valid RFC 2822 date string.

userStories.title: Add unit test for setSessionStorage in storage.ts
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want a unit test for setSessionStorage so that all exported storage utilities have test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover setSessionStorage: sets value in sessionStorage, handles SecurityError gracefully (returns false)
Test file is platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.test.ts (extend existing file)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The function is in platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts (line ~45). setSessionStorage has the same try-catch SecurityError pattern as setLocalStorage which IS tested. The existing test file platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.test.ts uses happy-dom for DOM APIs — add a new describe block for setSessionStorage following the exact same pattern as the setLocalStorage tests. This is a small, straightforward addition.
